benefit: Speed and memory from optimized attention without extra package dependency.
quality_impact: None expected beyond normal numerical differences.
risk: Low. SDPA API itself is marked beta in PyTorch, but Diffusers uses it by default on PyTorch >=2.0.
conflicts: Can be overridden by explicit backend context; verify backend availability.

benefit: Can improve attention speed and memory.
risk: Medium. Pip wheels can require newest PyTorch; build/source friction; dependency churn.
conflicts: May conflict with pinned Torch/CUDA; do not auto-install or import at boot.

benefit: Potential attention speed/memory improvements.
risk: Medium/high. Backend package and kernel support vary; FlashAttention-3 is Hopper-focused.
conflicts: Do not expose FlashAttention-3 for Ada as a default path.

benefit: Quantized attention may improve speed.
quality_impact: Possible because attention internals are quantized.
risk: High until benchmarked with visual receipts.
conflicts: Should not combine blindly with quantized denoiser or compile.

benefit: Can improve memory access and inference speed.
risk: Low/medium. Some custom modules may not support it cleanly.
conflicts: Apply per component and fallback on exception.

benefit: Steady-state speedup for repeated same-shape generation.
risk: High. First compile latency; graph breaks; recompilation on shape/LoRA changes; Windows GPU risk.
conflicts: Dynamic resolution; dynamic LoRA ranks/layers; unsupported graph operations.

benefit: Can reduce cold compile time while preserving much runtime speedup.
risk: Medium/high. Still compile infrastructure; shape and dependency sensitive.
conflicts: Same as compile, but less broad graph exposure.

benefit: Reduces CPU launch overhead for static workloads.
risk: High if done manually; static shape/control-flow restrictions.
conflicts: Shape changes, syncs, forbidden operations, dynamic control flow.

benefit: Reduces multi-image VAE decode peak memory.
risk: Low. Little benefit for batch=1.
conflicts: Unsupported by a few Autoencoder variants.

benefit: Reduces high-resolution VAE memory.
quality_impact: Can cause tile-to-tile tone variation or small differences.
risk: Medium because it changes output subtly.
conflicts: Quality comparison baselines; some autoencoders unsupported.

benefit: Can avoid SDXL VAE fp16 NaN/upcast memory behavior.
risk: Medium if silently substituted.
conflicts: Must match user expectation/model metadata.

benefit: Reduces VRAM with less transfer overhead than sequential offload.
risk: Medium. Stateful hooks; slower than full-GPU.
conflicts: Manual device moves after hooks; component reuse outside expected order.

benefit: Max VRAM reduction.
risk: High usability cost; Diffusers warns it can be extremely slow and impractical.
conflicts: Must be called before moving pipeline to CUDA.

benefit: Middle ground between model and sequential offload; stream overlap can help.
risk: Medium/high. Model implementation/device casting caveats; CPU RAM can increase.
conflicts: Potential device mismatch with some hooks and tiled VAE paths.

benefit: Potential large speedup after engine build.
quality_impact: May change numerics, especially with quantized engines.
risk: High. Engine build time; resolution/batch specificity; LoRA conversion; heavy dependency.
conflicts: Dynamic resolutions, unconverted LoRA, unsupported pipelines.

benefit: May provide TensorRT performance while preserving PyTorch workflow.
quality_impact: Potential numerical differences; FP8 variants change precision.
risk: Medium/high for local consumer app until tested.
conflicts: Dependency/runtime support; model graph support; dynamic workflow complexity.

benefit: Potential kernel fusions and attention optimizations.
risk: High maintenance burden because referenced path is deprecated.
conflicts: Export fragility; model conversion; separate runtime.

benefit: Memory reduction for Linear-heavy components.
risk: Medium/high if marketed as whole-pipeline SD quantization.
conflicts: VAE/CLIP not good targets; LoRA and compile interactions require tests.

benefit: Potential speed and memory gains on Ada/Hopper for supported modules.
risk: Medium/high. PyTorch version and component support constraints.
conflicts: Classic conv-heavy UNet support is not the same as Linear-heavy transformers.

benefit: Versatile quantization experiments.
risk: High for production because repository states maintenance mode.
conflicts: Prefer torchao/bitsandbytes for active production work.

benefit: Load community low-bit models while preserving low memory storage.
quality_impact: Dynamic dequantization and CUDA kernels can cause visual differences.
risk: Medium. Pipeline loading limitations and config requirements.
conflicts: Not a direct replacement for normal Diffusers-format folders.

benefit: 2–8 step generation.
quality_impact: Different distilled model behavior; negative prompts/CFG differ.
risk: Medium if hidden as normal scheduler.
conflicts: Requires LCMScheduler and method-specific guidance values.

benefit: 1–4 step generation.
quality_impact: Distilled model; CFG=0; no normal negative prompt.
risk: Medium if confused with SDXL base.
conflicts: Normal CFG/negative prompt UI; resolution expectations.
status: Fast Mode beta as separate model family

benefit: 1/2/4/8-step SDXL generation.
risk: Medium/high for wrong checkpoint/step combos.
conflicts: Must match steps and scheduler trailing spacing; 1-step less stable.

benefit: Low-step generation with LoRA/checkpoint variants.
risk: Medium/high due many recipes and moving model cards.
conflicts: Wrong scheduler/CFG/timestep pairing causes poor output.

benefit: Inference-time quality/detail changes.
risk: Medium; parameter-sensitive.
conflicts: Should not be hidden in default profile.

benefit: Quality improvements without external model/training.
risk: Medium; pipeline/layer support limited.
conflicts: Unsupported pipelines; layer regex mistakes.

benefit: Higher visual fidelity/details.
quality_impact: Intentional output change and latency increase.
risk: Low/medium; more VRAM/time.
conflicts: Pipeline component lifecycle and denoising split settings.

benefit: Better inpaint semantics and masked-region quality.
quality_impact: Improves target workflow; model output changes.
risk: Low; asset availability/model-family match.
conflicts: Base checkpoint fallback should be labeled lower quality.

benefit: Better spatial control.
risk: Medium due optional dependencies and preprocessor quality.
conflicts: Wrong model family; too many controls; scale conflicts.
